name: Civil Engineering
description: A comprehensive program covering structural, geotechnical, transportation, environmental, and construction engineering. Prepares students for careers in infrastructure development, construction, and urban planning.

subjects.name: Engineering Mechanics
subjects.description: Fundamentals of statics and dynamics including force systems, equilibrium, centroids, moment of inertia, and friction. Covers analysis of trusses, frames, and machines with applications to civil structures.
learning_outcomes: Analyze forces in trusses, frames, and beams using equilibrium equations
Calculate centroids and moments of inertia for composite sections
Solve problems involving friction and its application to civil structures
Apply kinematics and kinetics to analyze moving bodies
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for all civil engineering disciplines including structural, geotechnical, and transportation engineering.

subjects.name: Surveying
subjects.description: Principles of surveying including chain, compass, leveling, theodolite, and total station surveys. Covers traverse computation, contouring, area and volume calculation, and introduction to GPS and remote sensing.
learning_outcomes: Perform chain, compass, and leveling surveys accurately
Use a total station for topographic data collection
Compute areas, volumes, and prepare contour maps
Apply GPS and basic remote sensing techniques for surveying
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for land development, construction layout, infrastructure projects, and GIS data collection.

subjects.name: Building Materials and Construction
subjects.description: Properties, testing, and applications of construction materials including bricks, stones, cement, aggregates, concrete, steel, timber, and modern composites. Covers construction techniques for various structural elements.
learning_outcomes: Select appropriate building materials based on properties and cost
Test construction materials according to relevant standards
Understand construction techniques for foundations, walls, and roofs
Evaluate sustainability of different construction materials
subjects.industry_relevance: Core knowledge for construction, material testing labs, and building material manufacturing industries.

subjects.name: Concrete Technology
subjects.description: Properties of concrete ingredients, mix design, fresh and hardened concrete properties, admixtures, special concretes, and quality control. Covers durability, non-destructive testing, and repair techniques.
learning_outcomes: Design concrete mixes using IS/ACI methods for specified strength
Evaluate fresh and hardened concrete properties through testing
Apply non-destructive testing methods for quality assessment
Design durable concrete for aggressive environmental conditions
subjects.industry_relevance: Fundamental for ready-mix concrete industry, infrastructure projects, and precast concrete manufacturing.

subjects.name: Structural Analysis
subjects.description: Analysis of statically determinate and indeterminate structures using moment distribution, slope deflection, energy methods, and matrix methods. Covers influence lines, arches, and cable structures.
learning_outcomes: Analyze statically indeterminate beams and frames using classical methods
Apply matrix methods for computer-based structural analysis
Draw influence lines and determine maximum responses for moving loads
Analyze arches, cables, and suspension structures
subjects.industry_relevance: Core skill for structural design of buildings, bridges, towers, and industrial structures.

subjects.name: Strength of Materials
subjects.description: Stress, strain, and deformation analysis of structural members under axial, torsional, bending, and combined loading. Covers Mohr's circle, beam deflection, column buckling, and strain energy methods.
learning_outcomes: Calculate stresses and deformations in axially loaded members and shafts
Draw shear force and bending moment diagrams for beams
Analyze beam deflections and column buckling loads
Apply Mohr's circle for combined stress transformation
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for structural analysis, foundation design, and material testing in civil engineering.

subjects.name: Design of Steel Structures
subjects.description: Design of steel structures following relevant codes including tension members, compression members, beams, beam-columns, connections, and plate girders. Covers limit state design methodology.
learning_outcomes: Design tension and compression members as per code provisions
Design bolted and welded connections for steel structures
Design steel beams, plate girders, and beam-columns
Apply limit state design principles to steel structures
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for multi-story buildings, industrial sheds, bridges, and transmission tower design.

subjects.name: Design of Reinforced Concrete Structures
subjects.description: Design of reinforced concrete elements including beams, slabs, columns, footings, and retaining walls using limit state method. Covers detailing, development length, and earthquake-resistant design basics.
learning_outcomes: Design RCC beams, slabs, and columns using limit state method
Design isolated and combined footings for given loading conditions
Prepare reinforcement detailing drawings for RCC elements
Apply earthquake-resistant detailing as per code requirements
subjects.industry_relevance: Core skill for building design, infrastructure projects, and consulting engineering firms worldwide.

subjects.name: Geotechnical Engineering
subjects.description: Soil properties, classification, compaction, permeability, seepage, consolidation, and shear strength. Covers stress distribution in soils, earth pressure theories, and slope stability analysis.
learning_outcomes: Classify soils and determine their engineering properties
Analyze seepage and permeability in soil masses
Calculate consolidation settlement and rate of settlement
Evaluate shear strength and apply earth pressure theories
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for foundation design, earth dams, tunnels, and slope stability in infrastructure projects.

subjects.name: Foundation Engineering
subjects.description: Design of shallow and deep foundations including bearing capacity, settlement analysis, pile foundations, raft foundations, and machine foundations. Covers site investigation and foundation construction methods.
learning_outcomes: Determine bearing capacity and settlement of shallow foundations
Design pile foundations including load capacity and group effects
Design raft foundations for multi-story buildings
Plan site investigations and interpret subsurface data
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for safe and economical foundation design for all types of structures and infrastructure projects.

subjects.name: Transportation Engineering
subjects.description: Principles of transportation planning, traffic engineering, geometric design of highways, pavement design, and transportation economics. Covers traffic flow theory, intersection design, and traffic control devices.
learning_outcomes: Design highway geometric elements including horizontal and vertical curves
Analyze traffic flow characteristics and design signal timing
Design flexible and rigid pavements using relevant guidelines
Conduct transportation surveys and travel demand forecasting
subjects.industry_relevance: Core discipline for highway agencies, traffic management, urban planning, and transportation consulting.

subjects.name: Highway Engineering
subjects.description: Highway planning, alignment, geometric design, pavement materials and design, highway construction, and maintenance. Covers bituminous and concrete pavements, drainage, and highway economics.
learning_outcomes: Design highway alignments considering terrain and geometric standards
Design bituminous and concrete pavement layers
Evaluate pavement condition and design maintenance strategies
Apply highway economic analysis for project evaluation
subjects.industry_relevance: Directly applicable to national highway projects, road construction companies, and infrastructure development.

subjects.name: Railway Engineering
subjects.description: Railway planning, track geometry, components, points and crossings, signaling, and traction. Covers railway station design, high-speed rail concepts, and metro rail systems.
learning_outcomes: Design railway track geometry including curves and gradients
Analyze track components and their maintenance requirements
Design points, crossings, and railway station layouts
Understand modern signaling and high-speed rail systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing demand with metro, high-speed rail, and dedicated freight corridor projects worldwide.

subjects.name: Environmental Engineering
subjects.description: Water supply systems, wastewater treatment, air pollution control, solid waste management, and environmental impact assessment. Covers water quality standards, treatment processes, and pollution control technologies.
learning_outcomes: Design water treatment and distribution systems
Design wastewater collection and treatment systems
Evaluate air pollution dispersion and design control measures
Conduct environmental impact assessments for development projects
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for water utilities, environmental consulting, waste management, and regulatory compliance.

subjects.name: Water Resources Engineering
subjects.description: Hydrology, groundwater flow, reservoir planning, dam design, and flood control. Covers precipitation analysis, runoff estimation, hydrographs, and water resource systems planning.
learning_outcomes: Analyze precipitation data and estimate runoff using hydrological methods
Design reservoirs and dams for water storage and flood control
Analyze groundwater flow and design well systems
Plan water resource projects considering environmental and social factors
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for water supply projects, hydroelectric power, irrigation systems, and flood management.

subjects.name: Irrigation Engineering
subjects.description: Irrigation methods, canal design, diversion headworks, cross drainage works, and command area development. Covers soil-water-plant relationships, irrigation scheduling, and water logging control.
learning_outcomes: Design irrigation canals and canal networks
Design diversion and storage headworks for irrigation projects
Analyze soil-water-plant relationships for efficient irrigation scheduling
Design drainage systems to prevent water logging and salinity
subjects.industry_relevance: Vital for agricultural water management, dam projects, command area development, and watershed programs.

subjects.name: Estimation and Costing
subjects.description: Quantity estimation, rate analysis, cost estimation, tendering, contracting, and valuation of civil engineering works. Covers specification writing, billing, and project budgeting.
learning_outcomes: Prepare detailed quantity estimates for buildings and infrastructure projects
Perform rate analysis and prepare cost estimates
Understand tendering processes and contract management
Conduct property valuation using various methods
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for project cost control, quantity surveying, contract management, and construction business operations.

subjects.name: Construction Management
subjects.description: Construction planning, scheduling using CPM and PERT, resource management, quality control, safety management, and project monitoring. Covers construction equipment, labor laws, and project financing.
learning_outcomes: Develop construction schedules using CPM and PERT networks
Plan resource allocation and optimize project duration and cost
Implement quality management and safety programs on construction sites
Apply project management software for construction planning
subjects.industry_relevance: Core competency for managing large-scale construction projects in building, infrastructure, and industrial sectors.

subjects.name: Town Planning
subjects.description: Principles of urban planning, land use planning, zoning regulations, urban renewal, and smart city concepts. Covers housing, transportation integration, environmental planning, and building bye-laws.
learning_outcomes: Prepare land use plans and zoning regulations for urban areas
Analyze urban growth patterns and plan for sustainable development
Design neighborhood layouts considering all amenities and services
Apply smart city concepts for urban infrastructure planning
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing demand with urbanization, smart city missions, and urban infrastructure development projects.

subjects.name: Earthquake Engineering
subjects.description: Seismic hazard analysis, structural dynamics, earthquake-resistant design of buildings and bridges, ductility detailing, base isolation, and seismic retrofitting techniques.
learning_outcomes: Perform seismic hazard analysis and design response spectra
Analyze structural response to earthquake ground motions
Design earthquake-resistant buildings using relevant codes
Evaluate and design seismic retrofitting solutions for existing structures
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for earthquake-prone regions, structural safety, and retrofitting of existing building stock.
